lesson_meta.title: Unit 1: My Pet || அலகு 1: எனது செல்லப்பிராணி
lesson_meta.subject: english

questions.question: What is the name of Valli’s pet?
questions.explanation: Valli's pet is named Chittu.

questions.question: What animal is Chittu?
questions.explanation: Chittu is Valli’s pet, which is a kid (a young goat).

questions.question: What is the name of the flower Valli meets?
questions.explanation: On her way, Valli meets a flower named Daisy.

questions.question: How does the Mango tree describe itself?
options: I am a big Mango tree
questions.explanation: Valli meets a Mango tree, which is a large tree that introduces itself as a big Mango tree.

questions.question: Where does the Ant live according to the Alphabet Jungle?
questions.explanation: According to the Alphabet Jungle, 'A - Ant: Lives in a hill.'

questions.question: Which animal lives in a cave?
questions.explanation: In the Alphabet Jungle, 'B - Bear: Lives in a cave.'

questions.question: In the Alphabet Jungle, where is the Elephant found?
questions.explanation: According to the Alphabet Jungle, 'E - Elephant: Is in the forest.'

questions.question: Which bird is found in the lake?
questions.explanation: In the Alphabet Jungle, 'I - Ibis: Is in the lake.'

questions.question: Name any two tech-related objects found in the classroom.
questions.explanation: Tech items in the classroom include the Smartboard, Projector, Laptop, and Television.

questions.question: What sentence structure is used to identify a book?
questions.explanation: Students learn to identify common items using the structure 'It is a...' (e.g., 'It is a book.').

questions.question: Who are the two friends Valli meets on her way?
questions.explanation: Valli meets a flower named Daisy, a Butterfly, and a Mango tree.

questions.question: Does Chittu like to eat fruits?
options: It does not eat at all
questions.explanation: Chittu's diet includes fruits, and she especially likes bananas.

questions.question: Name three things Chittu eats besides fruits.
questions.explanation: Besides fruits, Chittu eats grass, leaves, and roots.

questions.question: How many kites are seen in the farm?
questions.explanation: During the counting exercise, they see 2 Kites.

questions.question: How many birds are flying in the sky?
questions.explanation: According to the numbers and counting section, there are 4 Birds.

questions.question: Which animal is seen jumping in the farm (4 in number)?
questions.explanation: The counting list mentions 4 Frogs, which are animals known for jumping.

questions.question: What is the home of the Horse called?
questions.explanation: In the Alphabet Jungle, 'H - Horse: Is in the farm.'

questions.question: Name the object used to show movies or slides in the classroom.
questions.explanation: A Projector is a tech item used in classrooms to display slides or videos.

questions.question: What is the greeting used in the morning song?
questions.explanation: The lesson starts with a 'Hello Song' using greetings like 'Good morning' and 'Hello'.

questions.question: Which letter comes after 'H' in the Alphabet Jungle?
questions.explanation: The Alphabet Jungle covers letters A to I. The letter 'I' (for Ibis) comes immediately after 'H'.

sections.title: பாடச் சுருக்கம்
sections.content: This unit introduces Valli and her pet Chittu. It focuses on basic greetings, identifying objects in the classroom, numbers 1-5, and learning letters A-I through animal associations in the 'Alphabet Jungle'.

sections.title: பாடக்கருத்துக்கள்
sections.content:
• Greetings: Hello Song, Good morning.
• Alphabet Jungle (A-I): Associating letters with animals and habitats (e.g., A-Ant-Hill, B-Bear-Cave).
• Classroom Objects: Identifying tech items, furniture, stationery, and fixtures using 'It is a...'.
• Numbers: Counting objects from 1 to 5 in a farm setting.
• Characters: Valli and her pet Chittu (a young goat).

sections.title: தகவல் பேழை
sections.content:
▸ Alphabet Jungle (A to I):
• A - Ant: Hill
• B - Bear: Cave
• C - Cat: Tree
• D - Dog: House
• E - Elephant: Forest
• F - Fox: Hole
• G - Goat: Shed
• H - Horse: Farm
• I - Ibis: Lake

sections.title: கலைச்சொற்கள்
sections.content:
• Greetings: Words like 'Hello' and 'Good morning' used when meeting people.
• Tech Items: Modern classroom tools like Smartboard, Projector, and Laptop.
• Stationery: Items used for writing and studying like Pencil, Notebook, and Eraser.

sections.title: நினைவில் கொள்க
sections.content:
• Chittu is a 'kid' (young goat).
• Use the sentence structure 'It is a...' to name objects.
• Remember the homes/habitats of animals from A to I.
